tests.title: MAF sensor sanity at idle
tests.summary: Confirm the mass airflow sensor reads plausible grams-per-second for engine size and idle RPM.
steps: Warm engine to operating temperature with no load (A/C off).
Record MAF g/s, engine RPM, and calculated load on live data.
Compare MAF reading to service data or a known-good vehicle of similar displacement.
Snap throttle once and verify MAF rises smoothly without dropouts.
tests.caution: Do not touch the MAF sensing element with fingers or shop towels.

tests.title: Fuel trim snapshot (bank 1 and 2)
tests.summary: Use short-term and long-term fuel trims to decide if the fault is fuel, air, or sensor related.
steps: At idle, record STFT and LTFT for each bank.
Hold 2500 RPM steady for 30 seconds and note trim movement.
If trims are strongly positive, suspect vacuum leak or weak MAF; strongly negative suggests rich condition.
Clear codes only after the underlying condition is repaired.

tests.title: Misfire isolation — coil/plug swap
tests.summary: Move the ignition coil and plug to another cylinder to see if the misfire follows the part.
steps: Note which cylinder the ECM reports as misfiring.
Swap coil and plug with an adjacent cylinder.
Clear codes and run until the monitor completes or the code returns.
If the misfire moves with the coil, replace that coil; if it stays, inspect injector and compression.
tests.caution: Disable ignition and fuel per OEM safe-power procedure before removing coils on live fuel systems.

tests.title: Static compression test
tests.summary: Mechanical health check when misfire follows neither coil nor injector.
steps: Disable fuel and ignition per service manual.
Remove all spark plugs and install compression gauge.
Crank engine for 5–7 seconds per cylinder and record PSI.
Compare cylinders; variation greater than about 15% warrants internal inspection.
tests.caution: Keep throttle wide open during crank unless OEM specifies otherwise.

tests.title: Upstream O2 sensor response check
tests.summary: Verify the pre-cat oxygen sensor switches rich/lean when fuel trim is disturbed.
steps: Graph upstream O2 voltage or lambda at idle.
Introduce a controlled vacuum leak or propane enrichment briefly.
Confirm sensor crosses mid-range within a few seconds.
If flat-lined, test heater circuit then replace sensor if wiring is good.
tools: Vacuum pump or propane (trained use only)
tests.caution: Use enrichment sources safely; never spray propane near ignition sources.

tests.title: Catalyst efficiency — O2 comparison
tests.summary: Compare upstream and downstream O2 activity before condemning a converter.
steps: Ensure no active misfire or rich codes.
Graph upstream and downstream O2 on the same screen at steady cruise.
Downstream should be steadier and lower amplitude than upstream on a healthy catalyst.
Repair exhaust leaks before converter replacement.

tests.title: EVAP small-leak smoke test
tests.summary: Locate very small vapor leaks after verifying the gas cap.
steps: Install a new OEM-spec gas cap or verify the current cap seals.
Close purge and vent valves per OEM test procedure.
Introduce low-pressure smoke at the canister service port.
Inspect hoses, filler neck, tank seal, and valve seats for smoke.
tests.caution: Do not pressurize the tank above OEM limits; use smoke, not shop air, on most systems.

tests.title: Thermostat and warm-up verification
tests.summary: Confirm coolant reaches regulated temperature in the expected time.
steps: Start cold engine and log ECT from ambient.
Note time to reach about 195°F (90°C) or OEM spec.
Compare upper and lower hose temperature after warm-up.
Replace thermostat if ECT plateaus low or warm-up takes excessive time.
tests.caution: Keep hands clear of cooling fans; fans may run after key off.

tests.title: Charging system load test
tests.summary: Rule out low system voltage before chasing module or sensor codes.
steps: Measure battery voltage key-off (should be about 12.4–12.7 V).
Measure voltage at idle with headlights and blower on (about 13.5–14.8 V typical).
Raise engine to 2000 RPM and recheck voltage.
Load-test battery if voltage is low at rest or cranking.

tests.title: Boost leak pressure test
tests.summary: Find leaks in charge piping that cause underboost codes.
steps: Install boost leak tester at turbo inlet or intercooler service point per OEM.
Pressurize to low spec (often 5–10 psi) — never over-pressurize.
Listen and spray soapy water at couplers, intercooler tanks, and BOV hoses.
Repair leaks and verify commanded vs actual boost on a road test.
tests.caution: Keep pressure low to avoid damaging MAP sensors or intercooler cores.

tests.title: EGR flow command test
tests.summary: Confirm the EGR valve opens and passages flow when commanded.
steps: Locate EGR valve and differential pressure feedback if equipped.
Command EGR open with scan tool at idle (may cause rough idle or stall — expected).
If RPM does not change, remove valve and inspect carbon blockage.
Clean passages and retest monitor.
tests.caution: Hot exhaust components; allow cooling before service.

tests.title: Parasitic draw — fuse bisection
tests.summary: Isolate battery drain by dividing the fuse panel until the offending circuit is found.
steps: Ensure all modules are asleep (doors closed, key out, wait 20+ minutes).
Measure current at battery negative cable with ammeter.
If draw exceeds spec (often < 50 mA), remove fuses in groups to find the circuit.
Narrow to single fuse, then identify consumers on that circuit.
tests.caution: Use inductive clamp or memory saver so modules do not reset excessively during testing.

tests.title: Fuel pressure — key-on static pressure
tests.summary: Verify the pump and regulator deliver spec pressure before replacing injectors.
steps: Install fuel pressure gauge on rail test port or adapter.
Key on engine off — note prime pressure and hold time.
Start engine and record pressure at idle and 2500 RPM.
Compare to service spec; low pressure points to pump, filter, or regulator.
tests.caution: Relieve fuel pressure safely; contain fuel spill; no open flame.

tests.title: Idle vacuum leak smoke test
tests.summary: Find unmetered air leaks that cause lean idle and high idle codes.
steps: Plug or cap the brake booster and PCV paths per OEM if required.
Introduce smoke at intake after MAF with engine off.
Inspect intake manifold gaskets, vacuum tees, and throttle gasket.
Repair leaks and perform idle relearn if needed.

tests.title: Cam/crank correlation check
tests.summary: Mechanical timing and sensor correlation for cam/crank DTCs.
steps: Scope cam and crank patterns if lab scope is available.
Inspect sensor connectors for oil contamination.
If correlation is off, verify timing marks before replacing sensors.
Document chain/belt service history.
tools: Scan tool PID for correlation if supported
tests.caution: Do not crank engine with timing cover removed unless service procedure allows.
